content.full_text:
BRUNICK, ALVAREZ & BATTERSBY
PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION

WILLIAM J. BRUNICK
DONALD ALVAREZ
MARGUERITE P. BATTERSBY
ROBERT D. BIDWELL
JAMES W. ANDERSON
DONALD F. CASH
STEVEN M. KENNEDY
FRANCES G. HERBERT

1639 COMMERCENTER WEST
POST OFFICE BOX 6425
SAN BERNARDINO, CALIFORNIA 92412
TELEPHONE: AREA CODE 714
889-8301 824-0623

REDLANDS, CALIFORNIA 92373
TELEPHONE (714) 793-0818

PLEASE REFER TO

April 20, 1990


Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: City of Yucaipa Request for Opinion

Honorable Members of the Commission:

Pursuant to Section 83114 of the Government Code, request is
hereby made on behalf of the City Council of the City of Yucaipa
for an opinion regarding the ability of two City Councilmembers
to sit on the City's Finance Committee, and more particularly
whether either Councilmember must exclude himself from advising
or making recommendations to the City Council on the City's
Budget or from voting on the Budget. The City of Yucaipa is
newly incorporated, and is currently considering its first
Budget, which includes contracts with the County of San
Bernardino for a variety of services, including law enforcement
services. Enclosed please find the City Resolution which
establishes the Budget Committee. The facts which we believe are
relevant to your determination are as follows:

Councilman No. 1: Wife is employed by the San Bernardino County
Sheriff's Department, as a Community Services Officer.
Her job duties consist of coordinating a variety of
community service activities in the City of Yucaipa. A
major City Budget item is the contract with the County
of San Bernardino for Sheriff's services, and Wife's
position is a line item in the Contract.

Councilman No. 2: Employed by the County of San Bernardino as
Finance Officer for County Special Districts, CSA
(County Service Area) 70, Zone CH (Chino Hills). Job
duties involve all accounting services for the Chino
Hills operation, including signing of all warrants on
agency funds and developing that agency's annual
budget. The Chino Hills operation is totally self
supporting, although it has its funds on deposit in the
Treasury of the County of San Bernardino. No general
County funds are expended by the agency, and the

FPPC: Yucaipa Request for Opinion
April 20, 1990
Page Two


compensation of this Councilmember does not derive from the
County's general fund.

As the Budget, including the Sheriff's Contract, is currently
under consideration by the City, we would appreciate your prompt
consideration of this request. Please contact the undersigned if
more information or clarification is required.

Thank you for your assistance.

Yours sincerely,

BRUNICK, ALVAREZ & BATTERSBY

Marguerite P. Battersby
City Attorney
CITY OF YUCAIPA

Enclosures

cc: Leslie Keane Stratton, City Manager
Mayor and City Council
CITY OF YUCAIPA

APR 17 '90 12:57 CITY OF YUCAIPA                                P.2


                         RESOLUTION  90-35


WHEREAS, it is desirable to form a Finance Committee to review the
financial matters involving the City of Yucaipa, and

WHEREAS, it is customary and appropriate to appoint members of the
City Council to serve in this capacity,

NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED the Finance Committee be formed as
follows:

1.  Membership – Two members of the City Council be selected to
    serve.

2.  Term – Members shall serve at pleasure of the Council.

3.  Duties and Responsibilities:

    A.  Review the financial operations of the City on a regular
        basis and not less than at quarterly periods during each
        year in consultation with the City Manager and/or the
        Finance Director.

    B.  Review and approve the warrant register.

    C.  Consult with the City Manager on budget matters and
        preliminary budget proposals.

    D.  When desirable and necessary report and advise the City
        Council on financial matters involving the City.


                                        ATTEST:

    ____________________________        ____________________________
    Gary R. Pitts                       Linda Drusys
    Mayor                               Deputy City Clerk
    City of Yucaipa                     City of Yucaipa

The City of Yucaipa is newly incorporated, and is currently considering its first Budget, which includes contracts with the County of San Bernardino for a variety of services, including law enforcement services. Enclosed please find the City Resolution which establishes the Budget Committee. The facts which we believe are relevant to your determination are as follows: Councilman No. 1: Wife is employed by the San Bernardino County Sheriff’s Department, as a Community Services Officer. Her job duties consist of coordinating a variety of community service activities in the City of Yucaipa. A major City Budget item is the contract with the County of San Bernardino for Sheriff’s services, and Wife’s position is a line item in the Contract. Councilman No. 2: Employed by the County of San Bernardino as Finance Officer for County Special Districts, CSA (County Service Area) 70, Zone CH (Chino Hills). Job duties involve all accounting services for the Chino Hills operation, including signing of all warrants on agency funds and developing that agency’s annual budget. The Chino Hills operation is totally self supporting, although it has its funds on deposit in the Treasury of the County of San Bernardino. No general County funds are expended by the agency, and the compensation of this Councilmember does not derive from the County's general fund. As the Budget, including the Sheriff's Contract, is currently under consideration by the City, we would appreciate your prompt consideration of this request. Please contact the undersigned if more information or clarification is required. Thank you for your assistance. Yours sincerely, BRUNICK, ALVAREZ & BATTERSBY Marguerite P.
